sutta: the buddha said for 1 who is virtuous in full possession of virtue there is no need for the purposeful thought may freedom from remorse arise in me this month is in accordance with nature that for 1 who is virtuous in full possession of virtue freedom from remorse arises now this uh word who is virtuous you could also say for 1 who has moral conduct in full possession of moral conduct there is no need for the purpose or intentional thought freedom from remorse arise in me this is in accordance with dhamma here they translate as nature that for 1 who is virtuous uh freedom from divorce arises so once you have virtue you don't have to think that you want to have non-remorse no remorse automatically follows them if you have see and love the buddha continued monks for 1 who is free from remorse there is no need for the purpose full thought may joy arise in me this monks is in accordance with nature that for 1 who is free from remorse joy arises monks for 1 who is joyous there is no need for the purpose full thought may delight arise in me this month is in accordance with nature that for 1 who is joyous delight arises monks for 1 whose mind is delighted there is no need for the purposeful thought may my body be calm this month is in accordance with nature that for 1 whose mind is delighted the body is calmed once for 1 whose body is calm there is no need for the thought may i feel happiness this month is in accordance with nature that 1 whose body is calm feels happiness monks for 1 who is happy there is no need for the god maybe be concentrated it follows that the happy man's mind is concentrated monks for 1 who is concentrated there is no need for the thought may i know and see things as they really are it follows naturally that 1 who is concentrated does so mounts for 1 who knows and see things as they really are there is no need for the thought may i feel revulsion made my passion fade in me it follows naturally that such a 1 feels revulsion and dispassion bounce for 1 who feels revulsion and dispassion there is no need for the thought you have to realize release by knowing and seeing it follows naturally that he who feels revulsion and dispassion realizes release by knowing and seeing so you see monks released by knowing and seeing is the object and profit of revulsion and dispassion revulsion and dispassion is the object and profit of of seeing and knowing things as they really are seeing and knowing things as they really are is the object and profit of concentration concentration is the profit and issue is the object and profit of happiness happiness is the object and profit of calm comes the object and prophet of joy joins the object and prophet of freedom from remorse freedom of from remorse is the object and prophet of moral conduct thus monks 1 state just causes another state to swell 1 stage just causes the fulfillment of another state for the sake of going from this from this side to the beyond that's the end of the sutta
commentary: the beyond of the further show refers to nibara so you see this sutta it says that 1 condition arises and another follows naturally without having to make a wish for it and this is a bit similar to 5.43 where the buddha said if you want certain things in this world for example to have long life to be happy to be wealthy and after that to have a good rebirth etc buddha said is no use praying for it or making a vow you have to walk the path that brings you to what you want to get for example if you want to have long life you must not kill if you kill then even you pray and you make about you still get short life because you don't have the condition necessary to give you long life so uh [Music] from this suta you know that you have to work for whatever you want to get and only if there's a condition for it you will get what you want to get for example if you want to be happy and to have a happy rebirth then you must give happiness to other beings and you deserve happiness otherwise there is no condition for happiness this is like a person if he sees the newspaper there's a vacancy for a doctor but he does not have the qualification of a doctor how can he apply for the doctor's job is enough however the person is applying for a lawyer's job he has to have the qualification of a lawyer before he can apply for a lawyer's job so in the same way whatever we want we got to have the necessary condition to get what we want so this suta is telling us if we want to be happy then we could have silla we have moral conduct so because we don't harm others then we have non-remorse and non-remorse leads to joy etc and the rest of this is a bit similar to the previous 1 so this is another suta that also shows if you want to [Music] attain liberation then you could have all these conditions that leads to liberation now we go to another suta is
aud_file: 086_Anguttara Nikaya Book 10A 101 - 108 by Bhante Hye Dhammavuddho Mahathera.mp3

items: for 1 who is virtuous in full possession of virtue there is no need for the purposeful thought may freedom from remorse arise
for 1 who is free from remorse there is no need for the purpose full thought may joy arise
for 1 who is joyous there is no need for the purpose full thought may delight arise
for 1 whose mind is delighted there is no need for the purposeful thought may my body be calm
for 1 whose body is calm there is no need for the thought may i feel happiness
for 1 who is happy there is no need for the thought may i be concentrated
for 1 who is concentrated there is no need for the thought may i know and see things as they really are
for 1 who knows and sees things as they really are there is no need for the thought may i feel revulsion made my passion fade
